topic.what: Risk-based three-layer framework for PQC migration in legacy systems combining diagnostic characterization, qualitative risk assessment, and quantitative ROI evaluation
topic.how: Field-informed approach grounded in empirical survey of large organizations across critical sectors, addressing legacy-specific constraints beyond generic cryptographic transition models
topic.why_matters: Existing PQC standards and migration guidance fail to address legacy-specific challenges including cryptographic rigidity, resource constraints, vendor lock-in, and regulatory barriers, creating critical vulnerabilities in quantum-ready transition

good_for: Organizations planning PQC migration in legacy-dominated environments
CISOs and risk managers assessing quantum-readiness
Critical infrastructure operators (finance, healthcare, energy, government)
Security architects designing context-aware migration roadmaps
not_for: Organizations with fully modern modular IT infrastructures
requires: Cryptographic asset inventory and dependency mapping
Stakeholder engagement across cybersecurity, IT, and governance functions
Regulatory landscape awareness (FIPS, Common Criteria, eIDAS)
Organizational commitment to phased migration timelines

novelty: First framework specifically addressing legacy systems as distinct PQC migration challenge requiring dedicated treatment
Field-informed methodology combining qualitative and quantitative risk models grounded in empirical survey data rather than theoretical assumptions
Three-layer framework bridging gap between crypto-agility assessment (CARAF) and operational implementation for rigid infrastructures
Integration of ROI-based analysis to support financial and strategic decision-making for migration prioritization
Unified approach covering entire migration lifecycle while addressing architectural, organizational, and regulatory constraints simultaneously
open_problems: Validation of proposed framework through applied research and real-world case studies
Development of practical tools, readiness indicators, and migration playbooks
Quantitative validation of risk models across diverse legacy system types
Case study implementation with financial institutions and critical infrastructure operators
Alignment with emerging regulatory requirements and sector-specific certification frameworks
Development of structured taxonomy for legacy system classification aligned with ITIL, ITAM, and CIS Controls
